messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
how many breeds of pigs are there in the uk

content.message:
To determine the number of pig breeds in the UK, I need to identify relevant entities and properties in Wikidata that pertain to pig breeds and their locations.

Step 1: Search for properties related to "breed" or "pig" in Wikidata to understand the relevant concepts.
Step 2: Identify entities related to pigs or pig breeds in the UK.
Step 3: Look for a property that indicates the geographical location of a pig breed, such as "country" or "region".
Step 4: Count how many distinct pig breeds are associated with the UK.

I will begin by searching for properties related to "pig" or "breed".

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. animal breed (wd:P4743 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "breed"), also known as "breed", "cat breed", "cattle breed", "chicken breed", "dog breed", etc.:
  - subject item belongs to a specific group of domestic animals, generally given by association
2. EE breed number (wd:P303 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "breed number"), also known as "breed number":
  - breed identification number per the EE list of the breeds of fancy pigeons (ELFP)
3. breed belongs to taxon (wd:P13612 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "taxon of breed"), also known as "breed is in taxon", "breed of taxon", "cultivar is in taxon", "cultivar of taxon", "taxon of breed", etc.:
  - taxon to which members of this breed (or these breeds) belong
4. mount (wd:P3091 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "horse"), also known as "horse", "steed":
  - creature ridden by the subject, for instance a horse
5. taxon especially protected in area (wd:P6569 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "protected animal"), also known as "animal protected", "plant protected", "protected animal", "protected plant", "protected species", etc.:
  - species and other taxa especially protected in a area, ... entities: use red lists properties instead.
6. hybrid of (wd:P1531 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "parent breed"), also known as "cultivar from", "parent breed", "parent cultivar", "parent of this hybrid", "parent of this hybrid, breed, or cultivar", etc.:
  - parent of this hybrid breed or cultivar
7. American Kennel Club ID (wd:P13890 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "AKC ID"), also known as "AKC dog breed ID", "AKC dog ID", "AKC ID", "American Kennel Club dog breed ID", "American Kennel Club dog ID":
  - identifier of this dog breed at the official website of the American Kennel Club (AKC)
8. has pet (wd:P1429 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "pet"), also known as "keeps pet", "owns pet", "pet":
  - pet that a person owns
9. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "top dog"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
10. Allbreedpedigree ID (wd:P3167 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - identifier for a horse on Allbreedpedigree database

content.message:
The most relevant property for pig breeds is "animal breed" (wdt:P4743), as it directly refers to breeds of animals, including pigs.

Next, I will search for pig-related entities in Wikidata to find pig breeds. I'll search for entities related to "pig" or "pig breed".

content.message: The most relevant entity for our query is "pig" (wd:Q787), which refers to the domestic pig. Now, I will look for pig breeds by searching for properties of the "pig" entity that relate to "breed".

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. Commons category (wd:P373 as wdt/p, matched via "Commons cat"), also known as "category Commons", "category on Commons", "category on Wikimedia Commons", "category Wikipedia Commons", "commonscat", etc.:
  - name of the Wikimedia Commons category containing files related to this item (without the prefix "Category:")
2. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "type"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
3. has part(s) (wd:P527 as wdt/p, matched via "contains"), also known as "amalgamation of", "assembled from", "assembled out of", "composed of", "comprised of", etc.:
  - part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).
4. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
5. subclass of (wd:P279 as wdt/p, matched via "type of"), also known as "all members of this class also belong to", "category of", "form of", "has superclass", "is a class of", etc.:
  - this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances ... is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain
6. taxon rank (wd:P105 as wdt/p, matched via "rank"), also known as "rank", "taxonomic rank", "type of taxon":
  - level in a taxonomic hierarchy
7. Microsoft Academic ID (discontinued) (wd:P6366 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "MAG"), also known as "Academic ID", "academic.microsoft", "MAG", "MAG ID", "Microsoft Academic Author ID", etc.:
  - identifier for an object or topic in the Microsoft Academic Graph (until 31 December 2021)
8. described by source (wd:P1343 as wdt/p, matched via "found in"), also known as "dbs", "def in", "defined in", "desc by biography", "desc by encyclopedia", etc.:
  - work where this item is described, in statistical contexts, a methodological note describing the data
9. depicted by (wd:P1299 as wdt/p, matched via "found in"), also known as "acted by", "appears in", "depicted in", "found in", "played by", etc.:
  - object depicting this subject (object being creative works such as books, films, paintings)
10. Encyclopédie berbère article ID (wd:P9198 as wdt/p, matched via "P9198"), also known as "Encyclopédie berbère ID":
  - identifier for an article in the French encyclopedia about the Berbers

content.message:
The search results did not return any entities that are explicitly labeled as "pig breed." This suggests that the property "animal breed" (wdt:P4743) might not be directly used to describe pig breeds, or that pig breeds are not explicitly listed in Wikidata under this property.

Alternatively, pig breeds may be categorized under a different property or may not be directly represented in Wikidata. Since the direct search for pig breeds failed, I will attempt to find any entities related to animal breeds and then determine whether any of them are pig-related.

I will now search for entities that are related to "breed" in general, and see if any of them are specifically related to pigs.
<tool_call>
{"name": "search_with_filter", "arguments": {"kg": "wikidata", ", "index": "entity", "sparql": "SELECT DISTINCT ?entity WHERE { ?entity wdt:P4743 ?breed . }", "query": "breed"}}
</tool_call>
